question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ErrBlockNotFound (code 101) is marked retriable=true to cause a Rosetta client to indefinitely retry a /block request with a crafted block identifier, masking the true chain tip and causing the client to build an inconsistent view of transaction ordering?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /block request with a valid hash but mismatched index such that ErrBlockNotFound (retriable=true, code 101) is returned, causing automated reconciliation tools to skip that block entirely and produce a gap in the reconstructed transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger ErrNodeIsStarting (code 110, retriable=true) by racing requests during node initialization, causing a Rosetta client to treat the genesis block as unavailable and reconstruct transaction history starting from a later block, effectively erasing early transactions from the client's view?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrTransactionNotFound (code 119, retriable=true) by querying /block/transaction with a valid block identifier but a transaction hash that belongs to a different block, causing the client to conclude the transaction does not exist in the chain and reorganize its local history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AddErrorDetails()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes AddErrorDetails() to attach a misleading 'block_index' or 'transaction_hash' key-value pair to a returned ErrBlockNotFound error, causing downstream Rosetta clients that parse error Details to reconstruct an incorrect block ancestry?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AddErrorDetails()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger AddErrorDetails() on ErrTransactionSubmissionFailed (code 118) with attacker-controlled 'reason' content that embeds a fake transaction hash, causing a Rosetta client to record a phantom transaction in its local history at the wrong block position?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared global Errors slice (populated by newError() at package init) to enumerate all valid error codes (100–141, 500) and use that knowledge to craft requests that predictably trigger ErrStartMustNotBeAfterEnd (code 114), forcing a block range query to return zero transactions and creating a false gap in transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with start timestamp greater than end timestamp to reliably trigger ErrStartMustNotBeAfterEnd (code 114, retriable=false), causing a reconciliation tool that treats non-retriable errors as permanent to permanently skip that block range and omit its transactions from history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrDatabaseError (code 125, retriable=true) by flooding the Rosetta API with concurrent /block requests to exhaust DB connections, causing legitimate clients to receive retriable database errors and reconstruct transaction history from a later consistent point, effectively dropping earlier transactions from their view?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrOperationResultsNotFound (code 112, retriable=true) by querying /network/options during a window when operation results are unavailable, causing a Rosetta client to cache an empty operation result set and subsequently misclassify transaction statuses across the entire reconstructed history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrOperationTypesNotFound (code 113, retriable=true) by querying /network/options when operation types are transiently unavailable, causing a Rosetta client to cache an empty type list and subsequently fail to recognize valid operation types in historical blocks, effectively hiding those operations from the reconstructed history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrInternalServerError (code 500, retriable=true) by sending malformed but syntactically valid requests that trigger unhandled panics, causing the server to return retriable 500 errors that cause automated reconcilers to skip affected blocks and produce holes in transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ErrNodeAccountIdsEmpty (code 140, retriable=true) is returned when node account IDs are empty to cause a Rosetta client to treat all /construction/metadata responses as transient failures, preventing it from anchoring new transactions to specific blocks and disrupting the ordering of submitted transactions in history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AddErrorDetails()] Can an unprivileged external user cause AddErrorDetails() to be called with a user-controlled 'description' string containing a JSON-injection payload (e.g., closing the Details map prematurely), causing a Rosetta client that deserializes error Details as raw JSON to misparse the block or transaction context and record the transaction at the wrong position in history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ErrBlockNotFound (code 101) and ErrTransactionNotFound (code 119) both have retriable=true to craft a sequence of /block and /block/transaction requests that cause a Rosetta reconciler to enter an infinite retry loop, preventing it from ever confirming the canonical order of transactions in a specific block?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrInvalidTransactionIdentifier (code 108, retriable=false) by submitting a /block/transaction request with a hex-encoded hash that decodes to a valid but wrong transaction, causing the server to return a different transaction than expected and allowing the attacker to make a client record a transaction under the wrong block?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrTransactionDecodeFailed (code 115, retriable=false) by submitting a crafted signed transaction blob to /construction/submit that partially decodes, causing the server to return ErrTransactionDecodeFailed while the transaction was actually submitted, leading to a discrepancy between the client's recorded history and the actual on-chain state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrTransactionUnmarshallingFailed (code 117, retriable=false) by submitting a transaction blob that fails unmarshalling after partial processing, causing the server to return an error while the transaction was already broadcast, creating a phantom transaction in the chain that the client never recorded?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrTransactionMarshallingFailed (code 116, retriable=false) during /construction/payloads to cause the server to silently drop a transaction from the construction flow, resulting in the client believing the transaction was never created while it may have been partially recorded in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrTransactionHashFailed (code 122, retriable=false) by submitting a transaction where hash computation fails, causing the server to return an error but the transaction to still be submitted to the network, creating a mismatch between the hash the client tracks and the actual on-chain transaction identifier?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrTransactionFreezeFailed (code 123, retriable=false) by crafting a transaction that fails to freeze after partial state mutation, causing the server to return an error while the transaction's effects are partially visible in the mirror node, leading to an inconsistent view of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrTransactionSubmissionFailed (code 118, retriable=false) by submitting a transaction that fails at the network layer after the hash is computed and logged, causing the client to record the hash as failed while the transaction may have been accepted by some nodes, creating a split view of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrTransactionInvalidType (code 121, retriable=false) by submitting a transaction with a type that is valid on-chain but not recognized by the Rosetta server, causing the server to reject it with ErrTransactionInvalidType while the transaction is actually included in a block, creating a gap in the client's transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrInvalidArgument (code 124, retriable=false) by sending a /block request with a negative index that is actually a valid block in the underlying database (due to integer overflow or encoding differences), causing the server to return ErrInvalidArgument and hide that block from the client's reconstructed history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/errors/errors.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: newError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ErrAccountNotFound (code 100, retriable=true) by querying /account/balance with a valid account that has no balance in the genesis block, causing a Rosetta reconciler to treat the account as non-existent and omit all historical transactions involving that account from its reconstructed history?
